reviews.review_text: Lovely staff, clean tidy amenities and well kept grounds.  Only downside was the colony of bats that have taken up residence nearby.

We stayed at a powered site during our time. It was beautiful, big double slab that was easy to reverse onto. There was good space between sites.
The park itself was very open, with plenty of accessible activities including mini-golf, playground, giant chess, lawn bowls, pool table, and swimming pool. The swimming pool is pretty bug, with several water jets and a mushroom. There is a large shallow area for young kids and good sized deeper section.
The camp kitchen was a highlight. There was four gas cooktops, an air fryer, and two BBQs outside. There was also another smaller cooking area near the camping section of the park.
The only thing to take into consideration is the presence of a fruit bat colony adjacent to the park. Our arrival at the camping section of the park was met with regular wafts of bat smell, not to mention the incessant chattering. However, we continued our 4 day stay and we found that the smell went away and the sounds became background noise that was easy to ignore. The presence of the bats was easily forgotten further into the park near the pool and camp kitchen.

I had a beautiful stay here with my family as we drove from Sydney to Brisbane. We enjoyed the wonderful amenities and made use of the outdoor grill during our stay. The staff is absolutely wonderful and helped me locate a piece of jewelry that I lost and even mailed it back to me in Sydney. Insanely grateful for their help and will be staying here again in the future!
Nice caravan park, good facilities, camp kitchen, putt putt golf, lawn bowls, playground, swimming pool, close to the river, close to Macksville or Nambucca Heads! 2min off the M1 motorway

This caravan park has so much going for it.  Unfortunately it disappointed us.  In the positive, the cost is good for a caravan site.  The location is great, being close to Nambucca Heads wonderful beaches and inlet and Macksville for groceries and pubs.  The pool is good for families with fountains and a baby play area.  They also have a couple of great Camping kitchens with BBQ's, enclosed cooking facilities and plenty of seating.  There is a bonus basket ball court, pool table and mini golf.  Fantastic, you say!  And we did too, until we realised that perhaps they are trying to save money by automatic check-in.  It was obvious that the most financial benefit for this caravan park were the permanent residents.  The rules for vans are that you need to come in after 12 noon.  One morning we were woken up by tyres reversing and going forward on the gravel next to us.  This was a 4.30 in the morning!  WTha?  People were backing into their van site at this time in the moring?  Waking up the nieghbours!  This is something that we have never experienced before.  There were other times that people came in  late at night and left early in the morning.  Obviously word has got around that you can come and go as you please and avoid paying.  We sent an email to the Caravan Park and suggested that a boom gate be installed like most caravan parks and heard nothing in return.  I do not think that the work ethics of Nambucca River Tourist Park warrant us staying again.  And I suggust you think seriously too.  Unless you want to avoid paying.

Such a lovely stay, and very quiet and calm. It was exactly what we needed on the lead up to Xmas.

The pool area was great for my 5 year old and not crowded like other campgrounds, being neurodivergent this is exactly what she needed.

The service was excellent and super organised for check in. We even left our phone charger there and they kindly posted in back to us. We are very thankful.

It wasn't an issue for us, but you do have to drive to either Macksville or Nambucca heads and not walk,  but you're in a easy place to drive to the best beaches and say trips.

Thank you, we had a great time and adored your resident water dragons.

I stayed at Nambucca River Park for two nights. TOPS place. The grounds lovely, pool  spotless and surrounds manicured.  The older cabin was very comfortable and clean, linen super sparkling white. The staff I cannot fault them so helpful, polite, Kahla, Kylie and manager Rachael just nice girls. Highly recommend the park. Will go again.
We believe that this is the best caravan park in the Nambucca area,all the facilities are outstanding ♥️👍

Here really feels like paradise.
The spacious camping sites, the clean and well-maintained public facilities, and the peaceful atmosphere make it a perfect place to relax and unwind.
What impressed us the most was how incredibly friendly and helpful the staff were.

We had a wonderful rest here, and we will definitely come back on our next trip.
if your staying in Nambucca and want to avoid the crowds a bit but still have immaculate fascilities pool etc stay here

We travelled with our 2 dogs and highly recommend the Nambucca River Tourist Park for many reasons. Their pet friendly cabin was spacious, with a covered fenced off verandah outside and even a long padded pet mattress running the length of the hallway inside the cabin. It was all well considered as the pet sleeping quarters had screened windows opening out to the verandah to keep the dogs cool.|Nursery Road is a quiet lane off the main road running along the river, with little traffic passing by, so a good place to walk the dogs, and the park has its own fully fenced leash free dog park which was empty each time we went. |The cabin was clean with a fully supplied kitchen, the shower was strong and the mattress excellent!  The park had its own pontoon across the road for water sports. And the other residents were so quiet, we only heard them in the morning when they drove out! |We would definitely stay here again.

Great place to stay if you're travelling with pets. The pet friendly cabin has a covered fully fenced verandah and pet sleeping quarters inside with a padded pet mattress next to screened windows to keep the dogs cool. There is a fenced off leash free dog park belonging to the Tourist Park which was empty each time we went there. The property sits on a quiet side lane off the main road next to the river so is a great place to walk your dogs. There is a pontoon for use, as well as putt putt golf and a pool. The cabin was spacious and clean, the kitchen fully stocked, the mattress excellent and the shower nice and strong. We would definitely stay here again.

Stayed one night on the way home, park in a good location and camping was in a large quiet area, park well maintained. Great facilities, well equipped modern camp kitchen, pool was lovely and clean and enjoyed using the pool table. The roomy bathroom with a twin shower was an awesome idea, would be great for families trying to shower a little one.

Outstanding service from Rachel and the girls, the  abun was clean and confortable !

Will be back soon !
A great place to stay. Great facilities, clean and well presented. Staff were helpful and friendly. A quiet and relaxing visit, we will most likely be back.

Overnight stay, $46 for 2 on powered slab. Park itself would get 5 stars as it was usual amenities plus many extras. Friendly staff. However, the 'experience' the review asks you to share is that it was awful. After I booked in and paid I was told to drive around works to get to site. I probably would have moved on if forewarned before paying. 20m away from site had a digger and skid steer moving earth from noon arrival until late in the afternoon so couldn't even hold a conversation outside. Had to batten down campervan to stop all the dust blowing in on a hot day. If four hours of almost continuous shrill reversing buzzer wasn't enough to give me a headache the fumes from an 8 tonne diesel truck idling  a dozen feet away for the last half hour certainly accomplished it. It's disgraceful to charge, or not warn, anyone to put up with that noise level for four and a half hours in such close proximity. PS, wifi doesn't reach sites. Saw a concrete pump arrive and took off in the morning.

Arrived not told prior to booking they had road works and maintenance OMG ALL DAY NOISE was HORRENDOUS ONLY 2 toilets and 2 showers for camping and they are share male female KITCHEN WAS BEAUTIFULBUT NO PLATES ETC ETC  EVERYTHING ELSE SUB STANDARD SHIT ON CONCRETE NOT SURE WHAT IT WAS BUT A LOT AND NO  WIFI  UNLESS YOU WALK TO CAMP KITCHEN I CANNOT RECOMMEND FOR THE PRICE AND THIS IS OFFPEAK PRICE $46 GO TO BIG 4 UP THE ROAD BETTER VALUE ESPECIALLY FOR KIDS  and campers
Caravan, good, a bit wet

I had an overnight here with my van travelling north. This is a lovely small park that is well maintained. Most importantly for me was the beautifully maintained small dog park that allowed my dogs a welcome break. All the trees allowed exploration and the covered seating area for me allowed me rest.
Staff very friendly and helpful
Leuke camping, veel faciliteiten en schone, warme douches.
Comfortable cabins and was a great place to break up the trip from Sydney to Brisbane with the grandkids.

Lovely park. Greeted by very friendly and helpful staff member. The amenities were dated but very clean. Swimming pool, mini golf, chess game and bowls all well maintained. Fantastic camp kitchen with plenty of cooking equipment and sparkling clean. Would definitely stay here again.

We arrived at 1.00pm and were allocated a cabin.  The floors were soaking wet (no attempt was made to dry them prior to our arrival).  The condition, size and very poor facilities made us realise that we made a big mistake booking into your Tourist Park.  We left at 5.00pm and drove home (4 hours drive) rather than stay in this place.

We booked this cabin hoping for a relaxing getaway, but the second we opened the door, we knew it wasn’t going to happen. The smell hit us immediately ,a strong, unpleasant mix of disinfectant and that stale, musty “old house” odor. It was overwhelming and honestly made it hard to breathe.||The place looked just as bad as it smelled. The lounge had visible stains, and everything inside looked like it hadn’t been updated (or properly cleaned) in decades. Old, worn-out furnishings, weird atmosphere , nothing like the cozy stay we were expecting.||We didn’t even unpack. We turned around and left within minutes. Definitely not worth it ,avoid this place unless you’re into disappointment and stale air.

The management is nothing but terrible! It is 9:00 PM at night and the blonde lady has abused myself and the children I am with! It was ridiculous and absolutely disgusting that we could not have some time with family and we have been quiet and kept to myself.
Bit far away from town to walk great park not crowded but only 2 lots of showers and toilet in amenities and very old but clean besides that was happy!

We had a wonderful stay at Nambucca River Tourist Park. The facilities were tidy and well stocked, plenty of activities for the kids, and the fully fenced dog park was a bonus. Concrete slab campsites helped with the wet weather.

Can recommend the Nambucca river tourist Park. We just stayed on a caravan site with our kids and they absolutely loved having all the fun facilities. What a great job!
The pool is huge and has a splash pad for the littlest kids.
Put put golf, bowls, giant chess and a basket ball court. There is also a fishing jetty.

The gardens were beautiful and
everything was super clean. Well done!!

The only thing letting it down was there was only two toilets for the park. It was done during the week when it wasn't busy but as the park filled up the toilets were not enough. Only two showers too.   Not a problem though I suppose if your van has one in it.

You need to bring everything to the camp kitchens too as they are not stocked with frypans or cooking utensils.  None of the BBQs worked until I went and purchased a fire lighter myself. But hopefully they fix that soon.

We loved our stay and wouldn't bother looking for anything else if we visited Macksville again. We would also stay here over Nambucca heads too as the price is heaps better and affordable for families and I think the kids would prefer it with the facilities.

I stayed here for one night and whole day with my 2 kids and mum, we fully utilised all the amenities like the pool and playground took the kids for a long walk around.did some washing  the room is very comfortable we used this spot as a half way mark to Brisbane to rest up and continue the following day.
Comfy bed for mum and I and bunks for the kids
AWESOME AIRCON FOR THE HOT DAY WE ARRIVED.
Will definitely be going again when I come back down to Sydney to visit as my half way stop.
Great value for money and 5min drive to a Woolies.

Fabulous spot to stop in on my trip from Brisbane to Melbourne.  Communicated well along the way to make sure I could check in before 5.30pm. Clean cabin, clean amenities and a view of the river. Well laid out - caravan sites have concrete pads and ample grass spots for tents. After the rain, these were a bit muddy but there were some higher spots. Camp kitchens for campers as well as indoor campers. Pool is huge, lawn bowls, mini golf and chess set to keep kids occupied for a bit. Nice park.
Lovely 4 night stop over at Nambucca River Tourist Park.  Really do not have to leave the park other then for supplies.  Jayco Wren Owners Group highly recommended if your are in the neighbourhood.

We stayed in a cabin overlooking the river. It was a pretty view. The cabin was clean and functional. There was a hiccup via booking agency but the manager was so helpful and accommodating. The cabin we  stayed in did only have a double bed which you forget how small it is once you've been in a queen. The air con was lovely and cold. It's a very convenient way to have a stopover if your driving to Sydney from Brisbane as it's the halfway point.

Parked our van on cement slab, very roomy and surrounded by green grass. Staff were great and shared details about events in Macksville. Bathrooms were clean, though dated, which didn't bother us. Nice camp kitchen and outdoor BBQ areas. We enjoyed our stay and would return when back in the area.

If your looking for peace & quiet, a well maintained site & clean cabins, this place is it. Staff are Awesome, very friendly & helpful.  Definitely wouldn't hesitate to make it my first point of stay if down that way again.

Great location. Camp kitchen is great. Amenities do the job. Pool and putt putt are great. I have loved coming here every year for the last 5 years. However, camping has gotten very expensive here and they no longer have grass camping sites due to flood regulations. Also, a bit of miscommunication and I set up camp on a grass site only to be very rudely awoken by the manager telling me to move my tent in not a nice manner. All other staff members were lovely and very helpful. I am sad to say I won't be staying again.

Very clean, the kitchen was fully stocked eating & cooking utensils & staff are wonderful.   It was so nice to have a billabong with wildlife to sit and zone out at.  Surprisingly close to Macksville & Nambucca Heads.
Not bad, will come again.
This place has very clean well maintained cabins. It has peaceful and pretty surroundings. The river is close by to do some relaxing fishing. I highly recommend staying here.
We have stayed in this park on quite a few occasions. The staff are always very helpful and amenities clean and grounds well maintained. Thanks to all we will definitely be return customers.
Nice park on the river

This was our 2nd stay at  Nambucca River tourist park. Both times in the on site cabins. The cabins are a Little dated, but clean and comfortable and suited our 3 night stay very well. Staff are great  and very helpful. We will defiantly stay again..
Well setup double slab powered sites . Friendly staff
Did not get to stay arrived just after 1700 reception was closed, phone the contact number on door was told park was closed no check in.
Es un asilo practicamente, caro! Very expensive caravan park. The cabins are no good for the 170$ the night.
Very pleasant
Lovely crew highly recommend
Stayed on a site plenty of room for car and caravan stayed here also in March very friendly staff would recommend
Great location, right on the Nambucca River. The park even has its own pontoon on the river. Perfect for a spot of fishing and the park has a friendly vibe. 😃👍
Awsome ❤️

Steer clear! Room was dirty had not been cleaned rooms passable but upon checking for blankets they were sweaty smelling and urine smells on sheets. Slime on sink, dusty floors only had been swept. We left early and didn’t sleep there. A very expensive and extremely bad experience
The place is very dirty don’t trust their pillow blankets toilets, very dirty
Nambucca is awsome
